semanticCore: Scattering, blowing away, winnowing, pulverizing, or wind-blow
quranicRelevance: Pulverizing mountains into scattered dust (Nasf - 'yansifuha rabbee nasfan'), and throwing calf ashes into the sea ('lanansifannahu fiy al-yammi').

keyDerivations.meaning: To pulverize, scatter, blow away, winnow, or reduce to dust

figurativeOrRareUsages: وَيَسْأَلُونَكَ عَنِ الْجِبَالِ فَقُلْ يَنْسُفُهَا رَبِّي نَسْفًا (18:105): And they ask you about the mountains; say, 'My Lord will pulverize them into scattered dust' (apocalyptic leveling).
discardedNoise: Obscure details of bird beak seed-cleaning.
summaryEn: The root represents absolute pulverization (Nasf), applied to the destruction of the golden calf (thrown as ash into water) and the eschatological leveling of mountains.
summaryAr: يدل هذا الجذر على التفتيت والنسف والتذرية بالريح، واصفاً سحق الجبال وتحويلها لرماد متطاير يوم القيامة (ينسفها)، ونسف ذرات العجل في البحر.
rawText: إِنْتَسَفَ 8 see 1. مِنْسَفٌ A vessel (وِعَآء) in which dates [and grain are shaken to remove the dust, &c. (TA in art. نفض.) نَسَفَتْهُ الرِّيحُ 1 , (Mgh, Msb, TA,) aor. ﹻ, (TA,) inf. n. نَسْفٌ; (Msb, TA;) and 🡻اِنْتَسَفَتْهُ, (TA,) The wind carried it away; (TA;) i. q. ذَرَتْهُ [q. v.]; (Mgh;) namely, dust. (Mgh, Msb.)
